title: HAIR Embassy HAL Generation Profile
description: An optional generation-oriented profile that binds HAIR structure, semantics, physical facts, and MCU topology into an auditable contract for Embassy-style HAL code generation. The same approved profile may lower either to an embedded-target Embassy HAL crate or to a separate host-emulated companion crate. Generated driver APIs must be derived from explicit referenced lowering inputs rather than from fixed placeholder method sets.

loweringPattern.description: Optional generator-facing lowering selector for cases where one supported driver kind has more than one approved evidence-backed lowering family with materially different bring-up behavior. Reserved values currently include `serial-jtag-preserve-link`, which selects a USB Serial/JTAG-style lowering that preserves an already boot-established link instead of synthesizing a reset-and-reattach sequence; `fsdev-pma-btable`, which selects a controller-level full-speed USB device family built around PMA/BTABLE-style endpoint state and attach/reset servicing; `counter-compare-timer`, which selects a timer-backed Embassy async timing family built around an explicitly modeled free-running counter plus compare/alarm path; `regular-sequence-adc-dma`, which selects a software-started regular-group buffered ADC sampling family backed by one explicit DMA path; `stm32f1-page-flash`, which selects an STM32F1-class memory-mapped internal flash sequencer with explicit unlock, page-erase, program, busy/error, and flag-clear bindings; and `legacy-event-i2c-master`, which selects a legacy event/status-flag-driven I2C controller-master family whose portable lowering is limited to 7-bit master transactions backed by explicit control/status/data bindings. Generators must fail explicitly if a selected lowering pattern lacks the referenced semantic inputs it requires.

timeDriverSource.description: Optional generator-facing selector for driver instances that claim capability tag `embassy-time-driver`. `systick` preserves the existing SysTick-backed time-base path on an interrupt driver instance. `hardware-timer` selects a timer-backed async time base on a timer driver instance when the document carries the explicit interrupt, semantic, structural, and tick-rate facts needed for delay helpers plus Embassy wake scheduling. `rtc` selects an RTC-backed async time base on an rtc driver instance when the document carries the explicit interrupt, counter, alarm, pending/clear, and tick-rate facts needed for Embassy wake scheduling.

timeDriverTickHz.description: Optional generator-facing Embassy tick rate for driver instances that claim capability tag `embassy-time-driver`. This must be present for `timeDriverSource = "hardware-timer"` and `timeDriverSource = "rtc"` so generated Cargo metadata and runtime behavior agree on the modeled time-base frequency. It must be omitted for `systick`, whose tick rate is defined by the SysTick lowering contract.

timeDriverBindings.description: Optional explicit time-base lowering bindings for driver instances that claim capability tag `embassy-time-driver` with a source such as `hardware-timer` or `rtc` and a lowering family that exposes one direct counter read plus one direct alarm/interrupt path. These bindings name the exact structural and semantic handles the generator may use for counter reads, alarm programming, interrupt enable, pending checks, and interrupt acknowledgement. Generators must fail explicitly rather than inferring these roles from vendor register names when this binding map is required for the selected lowering family but is absent or incomplete.

counterRef.description: Entity ref for the free-running counter register or field read by the generated time driver.

alarmRef.description: Entity ref for the compare/alarm register or field written when scheduling the next wake instant.

alarmApplyOperationRefs.description: Optional semantic operations that must run after writing the alarm/compare value when the timer family requires an explicit event, reload, or latch step before the new wake instant takes effect.

interruptEnableRef.description: Entity ref for the interrupt-enable field used to arm or disarm the wake interrupt.

interruptPendingRef.description: Entity ref for the interrupt-pending field read by the generated time driver before acknowledging a wake event.

interruptClearOperationRef.description: Entity ref for the semantic operation that acknowledges the wake interrupt cause used by the generated time base.

adcDmaChannelSampleTimeBinding.description: Explicit mapping from an ADC input channel index to the structural field used to program that channel's sample time for `regular-sequence-adc-dma` lowering.

dmaAsyncChannelBindings.description: Explicit async-completion bindings for one DMA controller channel. These bindings name the transfer-complete and optional half-transfer handles needed to expose Embassy-aligned IRQ-driven DMA futures on a `dma` driver instance.

channelRef.description: Entity ref for the DMA channel whose async completion state this binding describes.

transferCompleteFlagRef.description: Entity ref for the DMA transfer-complete status flag for this channel.

transferCompleteClearRef.description: Entity ref for the DMA transfer-complete clear/acknowledge handle written by the generated DMA interrupt handler.

transferCompleteInterruptEnableRef.description: Entity ref for the DMA transfer-complete interrupt-enable handle for this channel.

halfTransferFlagRef.description: Optional entity ref for the DMA half-transfer status flag for this channel.

halfTransferClearRef.description: Optional entity ref for the DMA half-transfer clear/acknowledge handle written by the generated DMA interrupt handler.

halfTransferInterruptEnableRef.description: Optional entity ref for the DMA half-transfer interrupt-enable handle for this channel.

dmaAsyncBindings.description: Optional explicit async-completion lowering bindings for `dma` driver instances. These bindings authorize the generator to expose IRQ-driven DMA completion futures only for the named channels and only when the same driver instance also carries the corresponding interrupt routes.

gpioExtiWaitLineBinding.description: Explicit EXTI-backed async wait bindings for one GPIO pin/line on a `gpio-port` driver instance. These bindings name the exact port-select, mask, trigger, pending, clear, and interrupt-route handles used by edge-triggered `embedded_hal_async::digital::Wait` lowering. Level waits may still be lowered from the GPIO input-sample path, but edge waits must fail explicitly when the selected pin lacks one of these bindings.

pinRef.description: Physical pin ref for the pin whose EXTI-backed wait path this binding names.

lineIndex.description: Controller-local EXTI line index used by this pin on the selected family.

portSelectRef.description: Entity ref for the AFIO/IO-mux field or equivalent selector that binds this EXTI line to the bound GPIO port.

interruptMaskRef.description: Entity ref for the EXTI interrupt-mask field that enables interrupt delivery for this line.

risingTriggerRef.description: Entity ref for the rising-edge trigger-enable field for this line.

fallingTriggerRef.description: Entity ref for the falling-edge trigger-enable field for this line.

pendingFlagRef.description: Entity ref for the pending/status flag read by the generated wait path before acknowledging a delivered edge.

pendingClearOperationRef.description: Entity ref for the semantic operation that clears or acknowledges the pending edge on this line.

interruptRouteRef.description: Interrupt route ref for the IRQ path that wakes this line. Shared-vector families still bind one route per line so the generator does not guess line-to-vector attribution.

gpioExtiWaitBindings.description: Optional explicit EXTI-backed async wait bindings for `gpio-port` driver instances that claim capability tag `embedded-hal-async-wait`. These bindings authorize the generator to expose the edge-triggered portion of `embedded_hal_async::digital::Wait` only for the named pins/lines and only when the same driver instance also carries the referenced interrupt routes.

i2cMasterBindings.description: Optional explicit master-transaction lowering bindings for `i2c` driver instances that select a family such as `legacy-event-i2c-master`. These bindings name the exact control, status, and data handles used by the generated portable 7-bit controller-master transaction path. When the same driver instance also claims capability tag `embedded-hal-async-i2c-master`, the binding map may additionally name the interrupt-enable handles used to wake async tasks from the same approved event/error route closure. Generators must fail explicitly rather than inferring these roles from vendor register names when the selected lowering family requires them.

dataRegisterRef.description: Entity ref for the data register field used for both address-byte and payload-byte transfers on the selected master path.

startRef.description: Entity ref for the control field written to request a START or repeated-START condition.

stopRef.description: Entity ref for the control field written to request a STOP condition.

acknowledgeRef.description: Entity ref for the control field that enables or disables ACK generation during master reads.

acknowledgePositionRef.description: Optional entity ref for a controller-local ACK/POS field used by families whose final-byte receive sequencing distinguishes the ACK position from the ACK enable itself.

busyFlagRef.description: Entity ref for the status flag that reports whether the bus is currently busy.

startConditionFlagRef.description: Entity ref for the status flag that reports completion of the requested START or repeated-START condition.

addressSentFlagRef.description: Entity ref for the status flag that reports completion of the controller-master address phase before any family-specific clear sequence is applied.

transmitBufferEmptyFlagRef.description: Entity ref for the status flag that reports transmit-buffer empty or data-register-ready state for the selected master path.

receiveBufferNotEmptyFlagRef.description: Entity ref for the status flag that reports receive-buffer not-empty state for the selected master path.

byteTransferFinishedFlagRef.description: Entity ref for the status flag that reports end-of-byte or byte-transfer-finished state when the selected family requires it for STOP/final-byte sequencing.

acknowledgeFailureFlagRef.description: Optional entity ref for the status flag that reports address or data NACK / acknowledge failure on the selected family.

arbitrationLostFlagRef.description: Optional entity ref for the status flag that reports arbitration loss on the selected family.

busErrorFlagRef.description: Optional entity ref for the status flag that reports a bus-error condition on the selected family.

clearAddressOperationRefs.description: Semantic operations that perform the family-specific address-phase clear or acknowledge sequence required after the address-complete flag is observed and before payload transfer may continue.

clearAcknowledgeFailureOperationRef.description: Optional entity ref for the semantic operation that acknowledges or clears the selected family's NACK / acknowledge-failure condition.

clearArbitrationLostOperationRef.description: Optional entity ref for the semantic operation that acknowledges or clears arbitration-lost state on the selected family.

clearBusErrorOperationRef.description: Optional entity ref for the semantic operation that acknowledges or clears bus-error state on the selected family.

eventInterruptEnableRef.description: Optional entity ref for the control field that enables the controller's event interrupt path for async master transactions.

bufferInterruptEnableRef.description: Optional entity ref for the control field that enables buffer-ready interrupts for async master transactions when the selected family exposes them separately from event interrupts.

errorInterruptEnableRef.description: Optional entity ref for the control field that enables the controller's error interrupt path for async master transactions.

flashBindings.description: Optional explicit internal-flash lowering bindings for `flash` driver instances. These bindings name the managed memory region, the portable erase/program geometry, the controller-local status and control handles, and the semantic unlock/lock/flag-clear operations required to expose a real `embedded-storage` NOR surface. They do not authorize option-byte, mass-erase, or vendor-specific fast-program helpers unless a later approved lowering family names those paths explicitly.

storageRegionRef.description: Entity ref for the structure memory region managed by this flash driver and exposed through the portable NOR surface.

eraseSizeBytes.description: Portable erase granularity in bytes for the managed flash region.

writeSizeBytes.description: Portable program/write granularity in bytes for the managed flash region.

busyFlagRef.description: Entity ref for the controller status bit that remains asserted while an erase or program operation is in progress.

endOfOperationFlagRef.description: Entity ref for the controller status bit that signals successful completion of an erase or program operation when the selected family requires an explicit completion flag.

programErrorFlagRef.description: Optional entity ref for the controller status bit that reports a program/sequence error on the selected flash family.

writeProtectErrorFlagRef.description: Optional entity ref for the controller status bit that reports a write-protect or access-protection error on the selected flash family.

programEnableRef.description: Entity ref for the control field that places the controller into program mode for memory-mapped writes.

pageEraseEnableRef.description: Entity ref for the control field that selects page erase mode for the managed storage region.

eraseAddressRef.description: Entity ref for the register or field programmed with the target page address before launching an erase.

eraseStartRef.description: Entity ref for the control field written to launch an erase after the address and erase mode have been prepared.

unlockOperationRefs.description: Semantic operations that perform the controller-specific unlock or access-enable sequence required before erase/program control writes are legal.

lockOperationRefs.description: Optional semantic operations that restore the controller's locked state after a portable write or erase operation completes.

clearEndOfOperationOperationRef.description: Entity ref for the semantic operation that acknowledges or clears the end-of-operation flag when the selected family requires explicit completion-flag clearing.

clearProgramErrorOperationRef.description: Optional entity ref for the semantic operation that acknowledges or clears the program/sequence error condition on the selected flash family.

clearWriteProtectErrorOperationRef.description: Optional entity ref for the semantic operation that acknowledges or clears the write-protect or access-protection error condition on the selected flash family.

adcDmaBindings.description: Optional explicit buffered-sampling lowering bindings for `adc` driver instances that select `loweringPattern = "regular-sequence-adc-dma"`. These bindings name the exact ADC and DMA roles the generator may use for software-started regular-group buffered sampling. They do not authorize injected-group or dual-ADC DMA behavior. Generators must fail explicitly rather than inferring these roles from vendor register names when the selected lowering family requires them.

prepareOneShotOperationRefs.description: Semantic operations that establish the fixed family-specific ADC and DMA mode bits for a one-shot buffered capture before the generator programs dynamic sequence/address/count values.

prepareCircularOperationRefs.description: Semantic operations that establish the fixed family-specific ADC and DMA mode bits for circular/continuous buffered capture before the generator programs dynamic sequence/address/count values. Required only when the generated API claims circular buffered sampling.

stopOperationRefs.description: Semantic operations used to stop or disarm the buffered ADC DMA path after one-shot or circular sampling when the family requires an explicit shutdown or disable sequence.

dataRegisterRef.description: Entity ref for the ADC regular-group data register read by the DMA path.

startRef.description: Entity ref for a single software-start control used to begin a regular-group conversion sequence when one direct structural write is sufficient for the selected family.

startOperationRefs.description: Semantic operations used to begin a regular-group conversion sequence when the selected family requires a composite start sequence such as arming an enable gate plus asserting software-start.

regularSequenceLengthRef.description: Entity ref for the regular-sequence length field programmed by the generated sampling helpers.

regularSequenceSlotRefs.description: Ordered entity refs for the regular-sequence slot fields (`SQ1`, `SQ2`, etc.) programmed by the generated sampling helpers.

channelSampleTimeRefs.description: Explicit mappings from ADC input channel indices to the sample-time fields programmed by the generated sampling helpers.

dmaTransferCountRef.description: Entity ref for the DMA transfer-count register or field programmed with the sample count.

dmaPeripheralAddressRef.description: Entity ref for the DMA peripheral-address register or field programmed with the ADC data-register address.

dmaMemoryAddressRef.description: Entity ref for the DMA memory-address register or field programmed with the caller buffer address.

dmaChannelEnableRef.description: Entity ref for the DMA channel-enable field used to arm or disarm the configured transfer.

dmaHalfTransferFlagRef.description: Entity ref for the DMA half-transfer status flag used by circular buffered sampling helpers when claimed.

dmaTransferCompleteFlagRef.description: Entity ref for the DMA transfer-complete status flag used by one-shot and circular buffered sampling helpers.

dmaHalfTransferInterruptEnableRef.description: Entity ref for the DMA half-transfer interrupt-enable handle used when the generated circular helper claims half-buffer interrupt-driven servicing.

dmaTransferCompleteInterruptEnableRef.description: Entity ref for the DMA transfer-complete interrupt-enable handle used when the generated buffered-sampling helper claims interrupt-driven completion servicing.

dmaHalfTransferClearOperationRef.description: Entity ref for the semantic operation that acknowledges or clears the DMA half-transfer event when circular buffered sampling claims half-buffer servicing.

dmaTransferCompleteClearOperationRef.description: Entity ref for the semantic operation that acknowledges or clears the DMA transfer-complete event.

executorIdleStrategy.description: Optional generator-facing selector for thread-executor idle behavior in checked-in or derived Embassy runtime harnesses that pair with this profile. `wfi` selects the default wait-for-interrupt idle path, while `spin` selects a non-sleeping idle loop for targets whose approved runtime/interrupt behavior cannot safely use WFI under the active critical-section strategy.

packageName.description: Base embedded package name for generated Embassy output. Host-emulated generation derives its package name automatically from this base in the first cut.

crateName.description: Base embedded crate name for generated Embassy output. Host-emulated generation derives its crate name automatically from this base in the first cut.

allOf.description: A generator-facing driver instance. Executable behavior must be justified by the referenced topology and semantic records plus structural register/field data reachable either directly or through explicit resolvable structural relationships. When lowered through host-emulated generation, every generated HAL-visible driver surface derived from this instance must also produce a paired emulator/state handle.

targetRef.description: For the first Embassy generator cut, this should resolve to a canonical block in profiles.mcuSoc that the driver instance lowers from.

modulePath.description: Generator-facing Rust module placement for the emitted driver/support code. This does not by itself define a fixed method set; methods must be derived from the referenced lowering inputs. Support-oriented placements such as a generated time module are allowed only when the referenced driver instance carries the explicit lowering contract needed for that support surface. The same placement governs host-emulated lowering for the paired HAL and emulator surfaces.

clockBindingRefs.description: Referenced clock bindings that may justify emitted clock-gating or bring-up helpers when those bindings and their linked structural/semantic data are sufficient for real lowering.

resetBindingRefs.description: Referenced reset bindings that may justify emitted reset assert/release helpers when those bindings and their linked structural/semantic data are sufficient for real lowering.

interruptRouteRefs.description: Referenced interrupt routes required by any emitted interrupt-driven API surface. Naming these routes does not permit the generator to invent unsupported async behavior.

dmaRouteRefs.description: Referenced DMA routes required by any emitted DMA-backed API surface. The generator must fail explicitly if a claimed DMA-backed behavior lacks the additional lowering data needed for real code generation.

pinRoles.description: Pin-role declarations used to bind generator-facing driver roles to explicit pin-topology routes. These support emitted pin/routing code only when the referenced routes and linked structure carry enough detail for real lowering.

initOperationRefs.description: Semantic operations that define emitted bring-up, calibration, enable, or other executable helpers. These are lowering inputs, not documentation-only labels, and should target the same hardware block as the driver instance.

stateMachineRefs.description: Semantic state machines that constrain emitted mode-transition helpers. These are lowering inputs, not documentation-only labels, and first-cut lowering expects the transition effect being emitted to target a field on the same hardware block as the driver instance.

capabilityTags.description: Generator-facing capability annotations. Capability tags do not authorize the generator to emit behavior that is unsupported by the referenced topology and semantic lowering inputs. The first-cut profile reserves `embassy-time-driver` to mark the unique driver instance that provides generated Embassy async timing support, `embedded-hal-async-wait` to mark `gpio-port` driver instances that may expose the `embedded_hal_async::digital::Wait` contract when explicit EXTI-backed wait bindings are also present, and `embedded-hal-async-i2c-master` to mark `i2c` driver instances that may expose the async `embedded_hal_async::i2c::I2c<SevenBitAddress>` master surface when explicit master bindings plus the required interrupt-route closure are present. The accompanying `timeDriverSource` selector determines whether that support is the existing SysTick-backed interrupt path, an explicitly modeled hardware-timer-backed path, or an explicitly modeled rtc-backed path; `timeDriverTickHz` fixes the Embassy tick rate for modeled non-SysTick sources; `timeDriverBindings` names the exact non-SysTick lowering handles when that path is used; `adcDmaBindings` names the exact direct roles for `regular-sequence-adc-dma` buffered sampling when that ADC family is selected; `dmaAsyncBindings` names the exact channel-local interrupt/flag handles for generated DMA completion futures; `gpioExtiWaitBindings` names the exact line-local selector, trigger, pending, clear, and interrupt-route handles for EXTI-backed GPIO waits; `i2cMasterBindings` names the exact legacy-I2C control/status/data roles and optional interrupt-enable handles for portable blocking and async 7-bit master transactions; and `flashBindings` names the managed region, geometry, control/status bindings, and required semantic operations for a portable internal-flash NOR surface.
